knowledge_atoms.content: Auto-Launch Workspaces provide deterministic workspace initialization triggered by opening a project, using configuration files to specify profile, mode, and initial prompts. This enables reproducible development environments, A/B testing across models, and reduced cold-start time.

Bidirectional Specifications evolve alongside code implementations, with agents updating specs as they discover codebase realities. This prevents specification debt and maintains alignment between intent and implementation.

BDI Hybrid Architecture combines Belief-Desire-Intent symbolic reasoning with LLM-based agents, providing explicit mental states for introspection and verifiability. Suitable for safety-critical systems requiring accountability.

4-Phase Spec-Driven Workflow structures development with explicit phases: Specify (requirements), Plan (decomposition), Tasks (generation), Implement (execution with verification). Achieves 56% faster development with 87% accuracy for multi-file changes.

Modular Agent Decomposition breaks agent systems into distinct subsystems (Retriever, Writer, Manager, Observer, core orchestration) with clear interfaces. Enables testable components and independent evolution.
Critic-Actor Pattern uses separate agents for generation (actor) and review (critic), with the critic validating outputs before commit. Catches errors before commit for high-stakes code generation.
Structured Human-in-Loop uses explicit checkpoints for human review using structured clarification tools. Prevents runaway execution and captures human intent for uncertain task boundaries.

Semantic Codebase Understanding builds comprehensive understanding through semantic analysis rather than file-by-file processing. Prevents context poisoning and enables drift detection for large codebases.
